sections.heading: Sector 1
sections.body:
Approach flat out, braking hard just past the 100m board, down to 4th gear. Turn 1 opens slightly, but turn two tightens dramatically. Trail brake into the apex and downshift to 3rd gear. Dont overslow the car — you want to stay tight and early on the throttle for a strong drive into the Esses. Avoid the inside kerbs, they can destabilise your car.

This is the heart of Suzukas rhythm. You are primarily in 5th gear, lifting and tapping the throttle through a rapid left-right-left-right sequence. Focus on flow, balance, and minimum steering input. Overdriving here will ruin your entire lap. The car must be planted and responsive — setup is critical. Stay tight to each apex and avoid scrubbing speed.

Flat or near-flat in 5th gear, depending on grip levels. Stay left on entry, gently feed in the throttle and let the car track out to the curb on exit. Its blind, so trust your inputs and commit early. Avoid the grass on exit — very easy to spin if you misjudge.

sections.heading: Sector 2
sections.body:
Degner 1 is a fast right-hand kink — a confidence corner. Brake lightly and turn in early in 5th gear, using the inside kerb. Exit wide and immediately brake hard for Degner 2, a tighter right-hand turn taken in 3rd gear. Hit the inside kerb and power out cleanly — dont run wide, the gravel is waiting for you!

One of the best overtaking chances. Brake hard uphill into this tight left-hander, down to 1st or 2nd gear depending on your gearing. Go for a late apex, and avoid turning too early. Be smooth on throttle on exit — its off-camber, and rear traction can vanish quickly. Get a clean run toward the Spoon Curve.

Flat out. Stay right, full throttle in 6th or 7th gear, and position yourself to attack Spoon. The car will be subjected to lateral load, so prepare early for the braking zone.

This double-apex left is one of the most technical corners on the calendar. Brake around the 50m board, down to 4th gear, and clip the first apex tight. Let the car drift mid-corner and then tuck in again for the second apex, lifting or lightly braking again and downshifting to 3rd. Traction is key here — a good exit leads into the long back straight. Use the exit kerb, but avoid spinning the wheels.

sections.heading: Sector 3
sections.body:
One of F1s most legendary corners. Flat out in 7th gear in qualifying trim. Turn in from the far right, clip the apex lightly, and trust your setup. In race conditions or on worn tyres, you may need a slight lift or tap of the brakes. Car placement is critical — run wide and youre into the wall.

Brake just before the 100m board, down to 2nd gear. Attack the right-left chicane aggressively but within track limits. Use the kerbs, but dont straddle them fully — its easy to bounce or spin over them. The final right kink must be taken cleanly to set up your run to the line. Get back on the throttle as early as possible.

DRS open, stay close to the right for a faster lap time or move left to defend. The long straight means every bit of exit speed from the chicane counts!
